title: Mnemonics for Nonprintable ASCII
text: Mnemonics for Nonprintable ASCII The control codes required by devices and function key sequences on keyboards often contain nonprintable ASCII characters (with values between 0 and 32 decimal) such as ^ESC, ^SOH, ^NUL, and so on. The translation table compiler recognizes special mnemonics for nonprintable ASCII characters. When a non-alphanumeric code (such as clear_screen ) is sent to an output device, it expects to receive control codes that represent this function. When a nonalphanumeric key, such as PF1, is pressed, the keyboard sends out a sequence of codes that represent this key. The documentation that is supplied with the device or keyboard should list the control codes or keyboard code required for these special functions and keys. Uniface ignores the case of the mnemonics, that is, uppercase and lowercase are equivalent. An optional single caret (^) can be used in front of a mnemonic to improve the readability of a device translation table.
